LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEC-04-2020 HEARING HELD RE: PLAINTIFF SATISH VEDAGARBA'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S PETITION (AND ADDENDUM TO SAID PETITION) TO VACATE AWARD OF ARBITRATOR. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF SATISH VEDAGARBA'S "MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER DENYING PLAINTIFF'S PETITION (AND ADDENDUM TO SAID PETITION) TO VACATE AWARD OF ARBITRATORS" IS DENIED. THE RECONSIDERATION MOTION FAILS TO RAISE NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS OR LAW NOT PREVIOUSLY AVAILABLE TO PLAINTIFF. (CCP 1008; EDMON & KARNOW, CALIFORNIA PRACTICE GUIDE: CIVIL PROCEDURE BEFORE TRIAL (TRG 2016) SEC. 9:324A.).) NO SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/RBU)

Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Plaintiff'S Petition (And Addendum To Said Petition) To Vacate Award Of Arbitrator & Court Reporter Fee Paid
Off Calendar Dec-04-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Plaintiff SATISH VEDAGARBA (Tel: 510-691-4896) appearing in pro per;
Atty. VICTORIA L. WEATHERFORD, Esq. (Tel: 415-393-8265) for Defendant AirBNB, Inc.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF SATISH VEDAGARBA's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Plaintiff'S Petition (And Addendum To Said Petition) To Vacate Award Of Arbitrator.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Satish Vedagarba's "motion for reconsideration of order denying plaintiff's petition (and addendum to said petition) to vacate award of arbitrators" is denied. The reconsideration motion fails to raise new or different facts or law not previously available to plaintiff. (CCP 1008; Edmon & Karnow, California Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2016) Sec. 9:324a.).) No sanctions are awarded.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Plaintiff'S Petition (And Addendum To Said Petition) To Vacate Award Of Arbitrator & Court Reporter Fee Paid

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-11-2020 Continued To Nov-18-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Requisition For Hearing Date For Petition To Vacate Award Of Arbitrators
Off Calendar Sep-25-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Satish Vedagarba (510-691-4896; vsattek@hotmail.com) Plaintiff in propria persona.
Victoria Weatherford (VWeatherford@gibsondunn.com) for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF Satish Vedagarba's "petition to vacate award of arbitrators" is denied.

Under both federal and state law, a court may vacate an arbitrator's award only on a narrow set of grounds. (9 USC 10(a); CCP 1286.2; Moncharsh v. Heily & Blase (1992) 3 Cal.4th 1, 12-13.) As the California Supreme Court teaches: "The arbitrator's decision should be the end, not the beginning, of the dispute." (Id. at 10.)

Petitioner says he got less discovery than desired and invokes CCP 1286.2(a)(5), arguing "refusal of the arbitrators to hear evidence material to the controversy." (Id.; see also 9 USC 10(a)(3) (to same effect).) To avoid "second-guessing" arbitrators, a petitioner must show "substantial prejudice" from a "refusal." (Schlessinger v. Rosenfeld, Meyer & Susman (1995) 40 Cal.App.4th 1096, 1110-11.) Here, the arbitration rules provided only for document exchange and witness lists; the arbitrator nonetheless allowed petitioner six interrogatories, drawing the line at depositions. Hence, petitioner got more discovery than he was entitled to, not less; he was not substantially prejudiced.

Petitioner also complains about arbitrator selection, but tethers this to no law. In any event, petitioner waived the argument by not raising it in the nine months the case was before the arbitrator.

order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
